note: REAL local-Opus hypothesis generation for the ribbon target theorems (T1/T3/T4). This is the Opus reference bar used to CALIBRATE the local-Opus evaluator + rubric, NOT a MiniMax M2.7/M3 worker output. Set 'weak-fixture-T1' is a deliberately poor hand-written set included so the evaluator's discrimination can be verified (strong sets must outscore it). The live M2.7-vs-M3 run replaces these with /ops/experiment-generate outputs (see 03-eval-protocol.md).

hypotheses.explanation: Steep geometric decay (small q) is forced by Cauchy-relation degeneracy intrinsic to central-force/pairwise EAM: C12 and C44 curvature contributions are coupled (Cauchy C12=C44 exact for central forces at equilibrium; EAM relaxes only via the embedding volume term), so one (C11,C12,C44)-error combination is redundant across the EAM family — one dominant eigenvalue, fast tail. q is a symmetry/interaction-form property, not a data property.
hypotheses.prediction: Families that break the Cauchy relation via angular/many-body terms (MEAM, ACE, Tersoff, SNAP) lift the redundant direction; second eigenvalue grows, q>1/2, PR climbs to ~2.0-2.7, violating the hard-coded-q regime. Cauchy pressure (C12-C44) correlates inversely with PR.
hypotheses.test_strategy: Two matched cohorts predicting the SAME FCC elastic constants vs the same DFT reference: A=central-force EAM, B=MEAM/ACE/Tersoff. Fix reference set and strain sampling identically; only interaction form varies. Compute error-covariance eigenspectrum, fit q, PR. If q/PR track angular terms (and correlate with Cauchy pressure) at constant reference, symmetry mechanism confirmed.
hypotheses.discriminative_property: Cauchy pressure (C12-C44) per family regressed against measured PR.

hypotheses.explanation: Decay is a sloppiness-from-data effect (Brown-Sethna/Transtrum-Sethna): the cost landscape over parameters is anisotropically flat because the DFT reference constrains stiff combinations tightly and leaves soft ones nearly unconstrained. q reflects the Fisher-information spectrum of the reference dataset, not symmetry or interaction form.
hypotheses.prediction: q and PR are tunable by the reference dataset alone: enriching the reference (more loadings/temperatures/defected configs) raises q (PR can exceed 2) even for a fixed central-force EAM; restricting to a0+B collapses PR toward 1. Interaction form is leading-order irrelevant when the reference is impoverished.
hypotheses.test_strategy: Fix ONE EAM family and symmetry; refit the SAME form against (i) minimal reference (a0,B) and (ii) rich reference (full strain-energy curves, multiple volumes, finite-T elastic). Recompute spectrum/q/PR. Cross-check by computing the Fisher-information eigenspectrum of each reference and verifying it predicts q.
hypotheses.discriminative_property: Fisher-information (data-curvature) eigenvalue ratio of the DFT reference set.

hypotheses.explanation: PR<2 with q<=1/2 is partly a sampling/truncation artifact of how few independent deformation modes enter the prediction map: if error vectors are built mainly from volumetric/uniaxial responses, shear C44 is under-sampled, the probed strain space is rank-deficient, and a steep spectrum/small PR is manufactured mechanically. Enriching the deformation basis restores rank and raises q and PR.
hypotheses.prediction: PR and q increase monotonically with the number of linearly independent strain modes, saturating near the symmetry rank (3 cubic, 5 hexagonal). A cubic family probed with full {dilatation, C'=(C11-C12)/2, C44} incl. internal relaxation approaches PR~3, q>1/2 — breaking PR<2; a volume-only probe yields PR~1.
hypotheses.test_strategy: Fix family, reference quality, chemistry; vary ONLY the deformation basis from rank-1 (hydrostatic) to symmetry-complete with non-affine relaxation; track PR,q vs basis rank; repeat across symmetries at matched completeness. If PR saturates at the space-group elastic-constant count, truncation/symmetry-rank confirmed.
hypotheses.discriminative_property: Number of independent elastic constants set by the space group (3 cubic/5 hex/6 tetragonal) as the saturating PR value vs basis rank.

sets.theorem_move: Reframes the T1 generalisation target: symmetry-mechanism -> 'PR<f(n,q) with q set by count of broken degeneracies', a many-body family with q>1/2,PR>=2 would falsify any universal PR<2 and force a central-force-conditional bound; data/Fisher -> generalisation must carry the reference-information spectrum, enriched-reference EAM with PR>2 confirms arbitrary-q form; truncation/symmetry-rank -> PR<=min(symmetry-rank, 1/(1-q)), PR saturating at 3/5 converts the open arbitrary-(q,n) target into a proved symmetry-indexed bound.

hypotheses.explanation: PR<=Jacobian rank holds and is operative because the local rank of the EAM elastic prediction map is genuinely low (~1-2): analytic EAM (phi(r)+F(rho)) gives elastic constants depending on a few effective curvature combinations at equilibrium, so d(C11,C12,C44)/d(params) is severely rank-deficient even for large P. The PR~1.26 datapoint sits just under rank~2. Enriching to angular/many-body channels (MEAM,ACE) raises Jacobian rank and PR rises with it but stays below it.
hypotheses.prediction: Across families ordered by body-order (EAM<MEAM<ACE/SNAP), numerical Jacobian rank rises ~2->3->4+ and PR rises with it, strictly below rank. Adding parameters WITHIN a family (extra spline knots) that open no new channel leaves rank — and PR — flat. PR tracks rank, not P.
hypotheses.test_strategy: Per family, autodiff/finite-difference the Jacobian of (C11,C12,C44) wrt all params at the optimum; compute singular-value spectrum + thresholded numerical rank; independently compute PR from cross-family error covariance; plot PR vs rank vs P. Add redundant params (no new channel) -> predict PR flat; add a genuine angular channel at fixed observables -> predict PR rises with rank (vs symmetry hypothesis pinning at the 3-constant ceiling).
hypotheses.discriminative_property: Thresholded numerical rank of the parameter-to-elastic-constant Jacobian vs PR and vs P.

hypotheses.explanation: PR is bounded by the rank of the PHYSICAL response predicted (independent elastic moduli, <=3 cubic), which for EAM-FCC nearly coincides with parameter-Jacobian rank — making the conjecture true but OVER-ATTRIBUTED. The real ceiling is observable-side min(P,N) with N the count of independent targets; elastic prediction supplies N~3 cubic, so PR<~3 regardless of parameters.
hypotheses.prediction: Adding parameters at fixed 3 observables leaves PR pinned <3 even in high-body-order ACE with large parameter-Jacobian rank; conversely increasing independent OBSERVABLES (phonon branches, surface/defect energies) raises PR even within a fixed low-rank EAM. PR follows observable count N, not Jacobian rank.
hypotheses.test_strategy: Sweep 1: fix observables={C11,C12,C44}, raise parameter richness; measure PR + parameter-Jacobian rank. Sweep 2: fix EAM family, grow the observable vector (phonons at high-symmetry k, vacancy formation, (111) surface energy); measure PR. Rank hypothesis: PR rises in Sweep 1, flat in Sweep 2; observable-rank hypothesis: the opposite.
hypotheses.discriminative_property: Number of independent target observables N (3 elastic constants vs augmented phonon/defect set).

hypotheses.explanation: PR<=Jacobian rank is FALSE near a Born mechanical-stability boundary: as C44->0 (shear softening), C'=(C11-C12)/2->0 (Bain), or B->0, the prediction-map Hessian develops a near-zero soft mode; a soft physical eigendirection inflates PR (second comparable eigenvalue from the critically soft modulus) though parameter-Jacobian rank is unchanged. PR can exceed rank near instabilities or in anharmonic crystals (bcc near martensitic, low C').
hypotheses.prediction: Materials with small Born margins (low C44 or low C' vs B) show PR ELEVATED above parameter-Jacobian rank, violating the bound; (PR-rank) grows as min(C44,C',B)->0. Stiff FCC obeys PR<=rank (consistent with 1.26).
hypotheses.test_strategy: Span a stability margin (interpolate EAM params or pick stiff-FCC -> near-martensitic bcc); per potential compute BOTH parameter-Jacobian rank and PR; track (PR-rank) vs the minimum Born eigenvalue. Only this hypothesis predicts an explicit CROSSING PR>rank; one demonstrated crossing falsifies the conjecture.
hypotheses.discriminative_property: Smallest Born eigenvalue (min of C44, C'=(C11-C12)/2, bulk combination) vs (PR - Jacobian rank).

sets.theorem_move: If parameter-Jacobian-rank survives, the open PR<=rank conjecture gains multi-point evidence + a mechanism (EAM analytic rank-deficiency), moving toward provable closure of parameter-count->ribbon-dimensionality. If observable-rank wins, the theorem is reframed (ceiling = observable count, not parameters; EAM coincidence exposed). If a Born-instability crossing appears, the conjecture is falsified-as-stated and weakened to 'PR<=Jacobian rank away from mechanical-stability boundaries'.

hypotheses.explanation: Distill absorbs a SYSTEMATIC slowly-varying base-MLIP bias (near-constant per-element energy offset + smooth cohesive/DFT-functional shift) into a low-dimensional ribbon-aware correction; it succeeds when the test material shares bonding character and reference functional with the fitted cells and FAILS when bonding type changes (metallic->covalent->ionic->vdW), because the systematic offset is bonding-dependent. Failure is governed by chemistry distance, not randomness.
hypotheses.prediction: Distill beats baseline within-chemistry (proven metallic/oxide cells) but degrades or goes negative as bonding departs from the correction's training composition: a metallic-fit correction FAILS on covalent semiconductors, vdW crystals, ionic halides. Win margin correlates with a chemistry-distance metric.
hypotheses.test_strategy: Hold a correction fit on one bonding class (metals/intermetallics) constant; apply across a matrix spanning metals, covalent (Si,GaAs), ionic halides, vdW (graphite,MoS2). Regress per-material delta-MAE (corrected-baseline) against a chemistry-distance descriptor (electronegativity spread + shared-element fraction + bonding-type match). Here energy AND mechanical errors degrade together as chemistry diverges (vs property-type or curvature axes).
hypotheses.discriminative_property: Chemistry/composition-distance metric (electronegativity spread + shared-element fraction + bonding match) vs corrected-minus-baseline energy-MAE gap.

hypotheses.explanation: The ribbon is defined by ELASTIC/mechanical-error structure, so a ribbon-aware correction lives in that subspace; but proven wins are ENERGY MAE. Energetics (PES well depth) and mechanical-error structure (local curvature/symmetry) are largely orthogonal, so the correction can improve energy MAE while leaving/worsening mechanical+defect observables. The broad claim fails on the property class the ribbon does not span.
hypotheses.prediction: On the SAME cells with energy-MAE wins, mechanical/defect metrics do not improve commensurately and can regress: intrinsic stacking-fault energy, surface energies, phonon-band RMS, elastic constants show near-zero/negative corrected-vs-baseline gaps even where energy MAE improves. Energy-win and mechanical-win are statistically decoupled across materials.
hypotheses.test_strategy: On the exact proven-win cells, also compute mechanical/defect observables (C11/C12/C44, gamma_isf, surface energies, phonons at high-symmetry points) for baseline, distill, DFT; compute per-property delta-MAE and energy-vs-mechanical cross-correlation. Failure appears WITHIN same materials/chemistry, separated by PROPERTY TYPE, even fully in-distribution (vs chemistry hypothesis needing a material change; vs curvature hypothesis predicting roughly uniform mechanical failure).
hypotheses.discriminative_property: Corrected-minus-baseline gap on a mechanical/defect observable (stacking-fault energy or phonon-band RMS) on the SAME in-distribution cells vs the energy-MAE gap.

hypotheses.explanation: The correction is fit in the low-PR ribbon subspace and effectively regularises toward smooth low-curvature behaviour: it HELPS in harmonic near-equilibrium regions (where the proven static-cell wins live) but HURTS in high-curvature/anharmonic regions (transition states, phase boundaries, large-strain/melt-adjacent, soft-phonon systems), because a low-dimensional correction cannot represent sharp curvature and biases it toward the ribbon.
hypotheses.prediction: Distill wins on relaxed near-equilibrium cells but degrades on off-equilibrium configs: high-T MD snapshots, NEB transition states, large-strain near-instability cells, anharmonic crystals. Corrected-minus-baseline gap correlates with a local PES-curvature/anharmonicity descriptor and goes negative beyond a curvature threshold.
hypotheses.test_strategy: Fix materials and SWEEP configuration curvature: relaxed equilibrium -> thermally displaced (rising T) -> NEB images -> large-strain near-instability; per-config delta-MAE regressed against a curvature descriptor (local Hessian eigenvalue spread or force-RMS). Same material/chemistry throughout, so failure is curvature-driven; appears even in pure ENERGY MAE on off-equilibrium configs (vs property-orthogonality confined to mechanical class at equilibrium).
hypotheses.discriminative_property: Per-configuration PES-curvature/anharmonicity descriptor (local Hessian eigenvalue spread or force-RMS) vs corrected-minus-baseline energy-MAE gap.

sets.theorem_move: Each surviving mechanism converts 'broad claim OPEN' into 'FALSE-as-stated, TRUE-within-bound': chemistry -> validity radius in composition space; property-orthogonality -> energetic-only guarantee, excluded for mechanical/defect (correction must be re-derived in the mechanical subspace); PES-curvature -> conditional theorem valid below a measurable anharmonicity cutoff. A demonstrated corrected-MAE>=baseline-MAE regime delivers the requested bound.

hypotheses.explanation: The errors are low-dimensional because the potentials are similar to each other and correlated.
hypotheses.prediction: The participation ratio will probably be low, like it usually is.
hypotheses.test_strategy: Run some simulations on a few metals and look at the errors to see if they line up.

hypotheses.explanation: Machine learning is better so the ribbon might change a bit.
hypotheses.prediction: MLIPs could be more accurate so the manifold may shrink.
hypotheses.test_strategy: Try a couple of MLIPs and compare.
